old_claim.note: Pharmacopoeia Compilation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Nineteenth-century chemical isolation retains chemistry and experimental pharmacology; Pliny's 77 CE encyclopedia is not a direct process input.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica defines alkaloids as naturally occurring organic nitrogen compounds and discusses their chemical properties and historical isolation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The chemistry account supports alkaloid isolation as a chemical practice without identifying Pliny's encyclopedia as a causal input.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected scope leaves this relationship outside the causal boundary. Nineteenth-century chemical isolation retains chemistry and experimental pharmacology; Pliny's 77 CE encyclopedia is not a direct process input.

ontology_before: alkaloid_isolation treated pharmacopoeia_compilation as a direct enabling relationship.
ontology_after: Nineteenth-century chemical isolation retains chemistry and experimental pharmacology; Pliny's 77 CE encyclopedia is not a direct process input.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: alkaloid_isolation no longer has pharmacopoeia_compilation as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A direct historical or technical source establishes pharmacopoeia_compilation as a necessary causal input to the newly scoped alkaloid_isolation claim.
short_reason: Nineteenth-century chemical isolation retains chemistry and experimental pharmacology; Pliny's 77 CE encyclopedia is not a direct process input.
